zh.lifeSummary: 當Catherine Howard Countess of Salisbury於1588年出生在英國艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登時，她的父親Thomas Howard -Earl Suffolk27歲，母親Lady Katherine Knyvett24歲。她於1608年12月1日與William Cecil 2nd Earl of Salisbury KG PC在英國米德萊可維斯特明斯特結婚。他們至少是9個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1673年1月27日在英國賀特福爾哈特菲爾德享年85歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Catherine Howard Countess of Salisbury于1588年出生在英国艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登时，她的父亲Thomas Howard -Earl Suffolk27岁，母亲Lady Katherine Knyvett24岁。她于1608年12月1日与William Cecil 2nd Earl of Salisbury KG PC在英国米德萊可維斯特明斯特结婚。他们至少是9个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1673年1月27日在英国賀特福爾哈特菲爾德享年85岁，埋葬在英国英格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Howard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, from the Middle English personal names Huward (also Howard) and Heward, from Old French Huard (itself from ancient Germanic Hugihard, hugi- ‘mind, understanding, spirit’ + hard- ‘hardy, bold’). As Hugh appears in Middle English as both How and Hew, this is the definite origin of Heward and a source of Howard. This surname is also very common among African Americans. See Hugh .

English: from the Middle English personal name Haward or Howard, usually an Anglicized form of Old Danish Hāwarth (Old Norse Hávarthr, from há ‘high’ + varthr ‘guard, guardian, warden’). Alternation between Haward and Howard may have led to later confusion with Hayward .
English: occasionally a variant of   Ewart   2.

variants.name: Fogarty
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, James Patrick, Kieran, Seamus, Siobhan.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Fógartaigh ‘son of Fógartach’, a personal name from fógartha ‘proclaimed, banished, outlawed’. It is sometimes Anglicized as Howard .

variants.name: Huggard
meanings: English: of Norman origin, from the medieval personal name Hugard, a variant of Huard (see Howard ). This surname is also established in Ireland (Kerry).

variants.name: Heward
meanings: English: variant of   Howard  .

variants.name: Hayward
meanings: English:

occupational name from Middle English hayward ‘keeper of the hedges and enclosures’ (Old English hēgweard), a manorial officer in charge of the common fields who protected corn and hay from cattle, theft, or damage, and supervised the harvest.
occasionally a variant of   Howard  .

variants.name: Huard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Aime, Adelard, Jacques, Jean-Pierre, Lucien, Marcel, Alcide, Amiee, Armand, Aurele, Clermont, Fernard.
French and Channel Islands (Jersey): from the Old French personal name Huart, either identical in origin with Howard 1 or a pet form of Old French Hue .
History: Jean Huard from Courson-Monteloup in Essonne, France, married Anne-Marie Amiot in Quebec City, QC, in 1670.

variants.name: Hugh
meanings: English, Welsh, and Scottish (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Hue, from ancient Germanic Hugo (ultimately derived from hugi- ‘mind, spirit’). This was a very popular name in England after the Norman Conquest, partly due to the reputation of Saint Hugh of Lincoln (1140–1200), who was born in Burgundy and who established the first Carthusian monastery in England.
Scottish and Irish: adopted as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic personal name Aodh, a derivative of a Gaelic word meaning ‘fire’. See Hughes .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 邱 and 丘, based on their Hakka pronunciation; see   Qiu   1 and 2.

nameMeanings.name: Catherine
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of   Katherine .  This form of the name is also used in France.

variants.name: Catheryn
meanings: Variant spelling of   Katherine .  This form of the name is also used in France.

variants.name: Katherine
meanings: Pet form of   Christopher ;  also of   Katherine   and its variants. In the medieval period it was a pet form of   Christian   and   Christiana .

variants.name: Catharin
meanings: Variant spelling of   Katherine .  This form of the name is also used in France.

variants.name: Cathrine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Katherine .  This form of the name is also used in France.

variants.name: Cathryn

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Catherine Howard Countess of Salisbury was born in 1588, in Saffron Walden, Essex, England, her father, Thomas Howard -Earl Suffolk, was 27 and her mother, Lady Katherine Knyvett, was 24. She married William Cecil 2nd Earl of Salisbury KG PC on 1 December 1608, in Westminster, Middlesex, England. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 27 January 1673, in Hatfield, Hertfordshire, England, at the age of 85, and was buried in England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Catherine Howard Countess of Salisbury est née en 1588, à Saffron Walden, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Howard -Earl Suffolk, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Lady Katherine Knyvett, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé William Cecil 2nd Earl of Salisbury KG PC le 1 décembre 1608, à Westminster, Middlesex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 9 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 27 janvier 1673, à Hatfield, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 85 ans, et a été enterrée à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Catherine Howard Countess of Salisbury(이)가 1588년 영국 에세스 사프런 월든에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Howard -Earl Suffolk(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Katherine Knyvett(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1608년 12월 1일 영국 미들레세스 웨스트민스터에서 William Cecil 2nd Earl of Salisbury KG PC(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1673년 1월 27일 영국 허트퍼드실르 하트필드에서 85세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드에 묻혔다.

ru.Westminster Abbey, London, Middlesex, En: лондон, лондон тоуншип, монро, Мичиган, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Catherine Howard Countess of Salisbury родилась в 1588 г. в местности саффрон валден, эссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Howard -Earl Suffolk было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Katherine Knyvett, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Cecil 2nd Earl of Salisbury KG PC 1 декабря 1608 г. в местности вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 27 января 1673 г. в местности хатфиелд, хиртфордшир, Англия в возрасте 85 лет и была похоронена в местности Англия, Великобритания.

es.Westminster Abbey, London, Middlesex, En: London, London Township, Monroe, Michigan, Estados Unidos
es.Saffron Walden, Essex, England, United K: Saffron Walden, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Catherine Howard Countess of Salisbury nació en 1588, en Saffron Walden, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Howard -Earl Suffolk, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Lady Katherine Knyvett, tenía 24 años. Se casó con William Cecil 2nd Earl of Salisbury KG PC el 1 de diciembre de 1608, en Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 9 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 27 de enero de 1673, en Hatfield, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 85 años, y fue sepultada en Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
